Cooperative Filling.name: Cooperative Filling
Cooperative Filling.description: The cooperative practice the Brotherhood was built to refuse. With a willing male partner who carries his own mana, the witch fills her reservoir directly through ingestion of his essence or being ejaculated into. A long downtime scene played out as intimacy; mechanically a meaningful refill from the partner's mana, larger than any single potion can match. Always done in private. The Queen's College consort-line still teaches it, though institutional fashion has drifted toward distilled potion-use over the last generation; direct filling reads as old-fashioned even in College halls. In conservative kingdoms outside Avenor it is doctrinally illegal; a witch and partner caught at it face arrest and quiet disposal.

Donate Mana.name: Donate Mana
Donate Mana.description: The consort's working: channel mana through cooperative practice into a bonded witch's reservoir or a shared working. The Queen's College trains and credentials its consort-line, though the role is increasingly old-fashioned even there as institutional fashion has shifted toward distilled potion-use. Done in private; the act is intimate when scenic and instantaneous in combat, and the consort feels a witch he is bonded to across a meaningful distance. Doctrinally unthinkable for a Brotherhood man; in conservative kingdoms a registered consort travels with discretion.

Co-Working.name: Co-Working
Co-Working.description: The cooperative practice in action: a witch and a willing wizard partner pool their reservoirs on a single working. The witch acts; the wizard's mana flows alongside her reservoir into the spell. Workings at the upper edge of either's solo reach become routine; workings beyond either's solo reach become possible. The classical Avenorian battlefield mode, taught at the College and still the most powerful application of paired practice; less common in everyday use as the consort tradition has narrowed.

Wild Draw.name: Wild Draw
Wild Draw.description: Mana-draw from a willing partner without mana of their own. Charmed, seduced, paid, or romantic partner; the act structured as intimacy rather than extract. Modest reservoir refill through a downtime scene, conducted in private. Requires carnal-arts training to calibrate; available once per scene per partner. Used by witches who lack potion supply or a consort, or who prefer the directness.
